summary: The paper is now a coherent negative-case study, but it still depends on a narrow audited slice, thin reviewer-facing positioning, and incomplete reproducibility packaging. The core scientific risk is no longer overclaiming a controller win; it is that reviewers may still read the paper as a single-case narrative whose broader audit-template value is asserted more strongly than demonstrated.

findings.description: The entire claim rests on a 100-example audited slice selected with a high-entropy prescan plus balanced remainder, so external readers can still question whether the negative pivot is robust or partly selection-sensitive.
findings.suggestion: State even more explicitly in the abstract and introduction that the paper is an audited slice analysis rather than a population estimate, and add one sentence explaining why this slice is still sufficient for the claim ceiling result.

findings.description: The manuscript claims value as a minimal audit template, but the evidence still comes from one DLM case study rather than multiple rewritten headline stories, so the template contribution risks sounding broader than what is shown.
findings.suggestion: Keep the template claim tightly scoped to this negative case and add a brief related-work paragraph clarifying that the paper offers a reviewer-facing audit pattern, not a general evaluation framework.

findings.description: The runtime disclosure is honest, but the paper still asks reviewers to accept a setup probe showing batch 28 while the audited arms remain at batch 8 on the eager path. That can create avoidable confusion about which runtime facts matter scientifically.
findings.suggestion: Compress the runtime story into one reviewer-friendly sentence: the audited metrics come from fixed eager-path runs at batch 8, and the later batch probe is only a disclosure artifact rather than part of the reported result.

findings.description: Scholarly positioning remains somewhat thin because the bibliography is minimal and several cited threads are represented more as signposts than as a deeply argued prior-art comparison.
findings.suggestion: Strengthen related work by adding one compact paragraph on why sham-control reinterpretation is the paper's specific contribution relative to DLM inference engineering, uncertainty-guided control, and sampler-evaluation work.

findings.description: The artifact inventory is strong, but there is still no explicit release or rebuild statement telling reviewers how the current-only bundle regenerates the tables, figures, and PDF package.
findings.suggestion: Add a short artifact-release paragraph or appendix note that names the current bundle, the key files, and the intended reproduction path for the main tables and figures.

metric_gaming_risks: A reviewer may still interpret the +7 net repaired margin over DNB-84 as the real headline and treat the sham-control failure as a secondary caveat unless the paper foregrounds the sham comparison earlier.

Because the paper operates on a curated audited slice, readers may worry that the localized gain survives because the slice is especially favorable to entropy-marked samples rather than because the audit template discovered a general failure mode.
novelty_risks: The minimal audit template can be read as a packaging discipline rather than a research contribution unless it is explicitly framed as a reviewer-safe interpretive intervention for small-gain DLM claims.
Without a sharper related-work contrast, the paper may be judged as an honest null-result note rather than a contribution that changes how similar DLM gains should be audited.
reproducibility_gaps: No explicit artifact-release or rebuild statement connects the current-only bundle to the final manuscript tables and figures.
The paper does not yet spell out whether the audited slice selection policy and manifest generation are sufficient for an external reader to rerun the packaging end to end.
Minor LaTeX appendix overflow warnings remain in the artifact inventory section, which can make the final package look slightly less polished than the underlying evidence deserves.
